title: Human in the Loop with AWS Lambda durable functions
description: Demonstrates how to integrate human review and approval processes into workflows using AWS Lambda durable functions. The workflow sends email notifications via Amazon SNS and suspends execution — without compute charges — until a reviewer approves or rejects via a secure callback link.

introBox.headline: How it works
text: A document submission triggers an AWS Lambda durable function that validates the input and sends a formatted approval email via Amazon SNS.
The workflow suspends using waitForCallback(), incurring no compute charges while waiting. The reviewer receives an email with approve and reject links routed through Amazon API Gateway.
A GET request renders a confirmation page to prevent email prefetchers from silently triggering approvals. The reviewer submits a POST to confirm the decision.
The callback handler enforces single-use by atomically marking the Amazon DynamoDB token as consumed, then resumes the durable execution with the approval result.
This pattern deploys one AWS Lambda durable function, one AWS Lambda callback handler, one Amazon API Gateway REST API, one Amazon DynamoDB table, and one Amazon SNS topic.
